note: Hand-authored front FEATURES, each with its own keyframe track and lifespan. kind=front: open CONNECTED contour (NORTH -> SOUTH, Baltic to Black Sea) as [lon, lat]; kind=pocket: closed ring around encircled forces ('encircled' says who is inside); kind=siege: closed ring around an invested city that still holds. Rings are authored open (no duplicate closing point) and closed by the ETL. 'from'/'to' bound a feature's lifetime: shown while from <= date < to, so 'to' is the first day it is gone (e.g. the surrender date); defaults: first keyframe date / open-ended for kind=front. Positions are schematic-but-grounded, placed to keep Axis-held cities west and Soviet-held cities east at each date — checked automatically against data/curated/city-control.json by build-fronts.mjs. Interpolated between keyframes at runtime so everything glides day-to-day.

keyframes.label: Leningrad blocked; Kiev salient about to be cut
Typhoon breakthrough: Orel taken, Vyazma/Bryansk pockets
Rostov retaken; high tide at Moscow
Winter line forms: Toropets wedge cut
Winter line: Rzhev salient, Demyansk, Toropets wedge
Into the Caucasus; toward the Volga
Uranus: flanks pierced, line still holds at the city
Uranus closes the ring at Kalach
After Stalingrad; winter offensive deep point
Winter offensive deep point: toward the Dnieper
Manstein's backhand: Kharkov about to fall
Kharkov retaken; Kursk salient; Rzhev evacuated
Kursk offensive spent; lines ~restored before Rumyantsev
Dnieper line held (Cherkassy, Kremenchug); Gomel lost in the north
Luga taken; Korsun reduced; Nikopol lost
Mud offensive: to the Dniester and Carpathians
Racing to the Vistula and Niemen
Lull before Jassy-Kishinev (front static on the Dniester)
At the Vistula; into the Baltics
On the Oder; East Prussia and Posen cut off
Berlin encircled; Elbe link-up at Torgau
Finns pushed back to the 1940 border
Goth line on the Taman peninsula
